---
title: "model and preds"
author: "John Curtin"
date: "`r lubridate::today()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true 
    toc_depth: 2
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
params:
  version: "v7"
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Set up environment
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)
library(Matrix)

theme_set(theme_classic()) 
source("https://github.com/jjcurtin/lab_support/blob/main/format_path.R?raw=true")

path_data <- format_path("llmpanel/data_processed")
path_chtc <- format_path(str_c("llmpanel/chtc/train_", params$version))
```

## Best config 
```{r}
folds <- read_csv(here::here(path_chtc, "output", 
                             str_c("batch_results_", params$version, ".csv")), 
                  show_col_types = FALSE) |> 
  arrange(config_num, outer_split_num, inner_split_num) |> 
  glimpse()

best <- folds |>
  summarise(rsq = mean(rsq),
            mae = mean(mae),
            n = n(),
            .by = c(hp1, hp2, hp3)) |> 
  arrange(desc(rsq)) |> 
  filter(hp1 <= .1) |> 
  slice_head(n = 1)

best$algorithm <- "glmnet"
best$feature_set <- "pref"

best
```

Fit model to full dataset
```{r}
source(here::here(path_chtc, "input", "training_controls.R"))
d <- read_csv(here::here(path_chtc, "input", "data_trn.csv"),
              show_col_types = FALSE) |> 
  glimpse()

d <- format_data(d)  
rec <- build_recipe(d = d, config = best)
rec_prep <- rec |> 
  prep(training = d)
feat <- rec_prep |> 
  bake(new_data = NULL)

model <- linear_reg(mixture = best$hp1,
                    penalty = best$hp2) |>
      set_engine("glmnet") |>
      set_mode("regression") |>
      fit(y ~ ., data = feat)

# model <- boost_tree(learn_rate = best$hp1,
#                         tree_depth = best$hp2,
#                         mtry = best$hp3,
#                         trees = 500,  # set high but use early stopping
#                         stop_iter = 20) |>  
#       set_engine("xgboost",
#                  validation = 0.2) |>  
#       set_mode("regression") |> 
#       fit(y ~ ., data = feat)

model |> write_rds(here::here(path_data, 
                              str_c("model_pref_", params$version, ".rds")))
```


```{r}
model |>
  tidy() |>
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") |>
  arrange(desc(abs(estimate))) |> 
  print(n = Inf)
```

Model coefficients  
```{r}
model |>
  tidy() |>
  filter(term != "(Intercept)", estimate != 0) |>
  slice_max(abs(estimate), n = 30) |>
  mutate(term = reorder(term, estimate)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.3) +
  labs(x = "Coefficient", y = NULL)
```

```{r}
tidy(model) |>
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") |>
  filter(estimate != 0) |>
  mutate(
    direction = if_else(estimate > 0, "positive", "negative"),
    abs_estimate = abs(estimate),
    feature_type = if_else(str_detect(term, "_x_"), "Interaction", "Main Effect")
  ) |>
  slice_max(abs_estimate, n = 15, by = feature_type) |>
  arrange(feature_type, abs_estimate) |>
  mutate(term = factor(term, levels = term)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = abs_estimate, y = term, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("positive" = "forestgreen", "negative" = "firebrick"),
    name = "Direction"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ feature_type, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = "Absolute Coefficient",
    y = NULL,
    title = "Feature Importance"
  )
```


```{r}
tones <- c("legitimizing", "self_efficacy", "norms", "value_affirmation", "acknowledging")
styles <- c("formal", "informal")

preds_id <- d |> 
  group_by(subid) |> 
  slice_head(n = 1) |> 
  select(-style, -tone)

conds <- expand_grid(subid = unique(d$subid), tone = tones, style = styles)

preds <- conds |> 
  left_join(preds_id, by = "subid")

feat_preds <- rec_prep |> 
  bake(new_data = preds)

assign <- feat_preds |> 
  mutate(predicted  = predict(model, feat_preds)$.pred) |> 
  mutate(tone = preds$tone,
         style = preds$style,
         subid = preds$subid) |> 
  select(subid, tone, style, predicted)

assign |> 
  group_by(subid) |> 
  summarize(min = min(predicted),
            max = max(predicted),
            dif = max  - min) |> 
  summarize(mean(dif), median(dif), max(dif), min(dif))
```



NOTE this gets best cross of tone and style.
Could do separately for tone and style
```{r}
assign_best <- assign |> 
  group_by(subid) |> 
  slice_max(predicted) |> 
  select(subid, tone_best = tone, style_best = style)

assign_worst <- assign |> 
  group_by(subid) |> 
  slice_min(predicted) |> 
  select(subid, tone_worst = tone, style_worst = style)

assign <- assign |> 
  left_join(assign_best, by = "subid") |> 
  left_join(assign_worst, by = "subid") |> 
  select(subid, contains(c("best", "worst")))
```


```{r}
top <- d |> 
  select(subid, tone, style, y) |> 
  left_join(assign |> group_by(subid) |> slice_head(n = 1), 
            by = "subid") |> 
  filter(tone == tone_best,
         style == style_best) |> 
  mutate(cond = "best")

other <- d |> 
  select(subid, tone, style, y) |> 
  left_join(assign |> group_by(subid) |> slice_head(n = 1), 
            by = "subid") |> 
  filter(!(tone == tone_best & style == style_best),
         !(tone == tone_worst & style == style_worst)) |> 
  mutate(cond = "other")


worst <- d |> 
  select(subid, tone, style, y) |> 
  left_join(assign |> group_by(subid) |> slice_head(n = 1), 
            by = "subid") |> 
  filter(tone == tone_worst,
         style == style_worst) |> 
  mutate(cond = "worst")
```

```{r}
all <- top |> 
  bind_rows(other) |> 
  bind_rows(worst)

all |> write_csv(here::here(path_data, str_c("pred_conditions_", params$version, ".csv")))

all |>
  summarise(mean_rating = mean(y),
            sd = sd(y),
            .by = cond) 
all |>
  summarise(mean_rating = mean(y),
            sd = sd(y) / sqrt(n()),
            .by = cond) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = cond, y = mean_rating)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_rating - sd, ymax = mean_rating + sd),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.25) +
  labs(x = "Message Category", y = "Mean Message Rating",
       title = "Mean Message Rating by Tone and Style") +
  ylim(0, 7) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))
```


And here is what people are assigned

```{r}
all |> 
  group_by(subid) |> 
  slice_head(n = 1) |> 
  ungroup() |> 
  unite(tone_style, tone, style, sep = " & ", remove = FALSE) |> 
  tab(tone_style)
```
